zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Massey of Puddington於1580年8月9日出生在英國爾伯頓時，他的父親John Massey34歲，母親Elizabeth Ashton32歲。他於大約1608年與Catherine Herbert在威爾士蒙格莫利夏爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1649年在他的家鄉享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Massey of Puddington于1580年8月9日出生在英国爾伯頓时，他的父亲John Massey34岁，母亲Elizabeth Ashton32岁。他于大约1608年与Catherine Herbert在威尔士蒙格莫利夏爾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1649年在他的家乡享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Massey of Puddington was born on 9 August 1580, in Burton, Cheshire, England, his father, John Massey, was 34 and his mother, Elizabeth Ashton, was 32. He married Catherine Herbert about 1608, in Montgomeryshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1649, in his hometown, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Massey of Puddington est né le 9 août 1580, à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, John Massey, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Ashton, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Catherine Herbert environ 1608, à Montgomeryshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1649, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Massey of Puddington(이)가 1580년 8월 9일 영국 체실르 버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Massey(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Ashton(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1608년 웨일스 몽고머이실르에서 Catherine Herbert(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1649년 그의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Massey of Puddington родился 9 августа 1580 г. в местности буртон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Massey было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Ashton, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Herbert около 1608 г. в местности монтгомиришир, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1649 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 69 лет.
